title: Does the cyclic group C_8835 admit a (8835,4417,2208)-difference set?
micro_paper_assessment: PAPER_READY micro-paper packet: the dossier supports a title-sized nonexistence theorem for a named residual cyclic Hadamard case, and the remaining work is bounded to citation pinning and note drafting rather than new mathematics.
family_affinity: very high; the proof attacks the exact residual cyclic Hadamard case using family-standard multiplier, character, and orbit-count tools.
generalization_signal: moderate to strong; the method should transfer to other residual cyclic Hadamard cases where one multiplier prime yields a rigid small-quotient profile and a sparse orbit block.
strongest_honest_claim: The bounded verify and publication audit support the exact nonexistence theorem that C_8835 admits no cyclic (8835,4417,2208)-difference set; equivalently, the unique mod 15 profile forces a 624-point {5,10} block that cannot be assembled from the available full 47-multiplier orbits.
paper_title_hint: On the Nonexistence of a Cyclic (8835,4417,2208)-Difference Set
candidate_theorem_slice: There is no cyclic (8835,4417,2208)-difference set; equivalently, the forced mod 15 profile yields a 624-point {5,10} block that cannot be realized as a union of full 47-multiplier orbits.
lean_gate_reason: Lean is a secondary formal-seal lane for this packet. The note is already human-ready on the bounded audit; the remaining publication task is clean citation pinning and prose packaging, not formalization.
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
